issues.title: SupabaseClient type alias re-declared in 8 infrastructure files
issues.description: The type alias `type SupabaseClient = ReturnType<typeof createBrowserSupabaseClient>` is copy-pasted into 8 separate infrastructure files across admin, studio, and payments. Only `apps/payments/src/shared/domain/types.ts` exports it as a reusable type; the others are private local aliases that replicate the same knowledge.
issues.fix: Export `SupabaseClient` once from `packages/api/src/supabase/index.ts` (it already exports `createBrowserSupabaseClient`), then add `export type SupabaseClient = ReturnType<typeof createBrowserSupabaseClient>` there. Each infrastructure file should import the shared type instead of re-declaring it. Alternatively, each app can define it once in its own `shared/domain/types.ts` (as payments already does) and the infrastructure files import from there.

issues.title: `useMemo(() => createBrowserSupabaseClient(), [])` pattern in 20+ hooks
issues.description: The one-liner `const supabase = useMemo(() => createBrowserSupabaseClient(), [])` is duplicated in at least 20 hook files across store, payments, studio, auth, and admin. Only `apps/admin/src/shared/application/hooks/useSupabase.ts` abstracts this into a reusable `useSupabase()` hook, but that hook is not used by the other apps.
issues.fix: Move `useSupabase()` from `apps/admin/src/shared/application/hooks/useSupabase.ts` into the `packages/shared` package (e.g. `packages/shared/src/hooks/useSupabase.ts`) and export it from the shared package index. All hooks across all apps can then `import { useSupabase } from 'shared'` instead of repeating the one-liner.

issues.title: Cart cookie key `"candystore-cart"` defined twice across apps
issues.description: The cart cookie key string `"candystore-cart"` is defined as `COOKIE_KEY` in the store app and independently as `CART_COOKIE_KEY` in `apps/payments/src/shared/domain/constants.ts`. Both represent the same piece of knowledge (the shared cross-app cookie name). If the cookie name changes, it must be updated in two places.
issues.fix: Define the canonical constant once — the best place is `packages/shared/src/constants/` since this value is genuinely cross-app. Export it as `CART_COOKIE_KEY` and import it in both the store's `cartCookiePersistence.ts` and the payments app's constants. The payments `shared/domain/constants.ts` already has the right name; the store should import from the shared package instead of redeclaring `COOKIE_KEY`.

issues.title: `getSharedCookieDomain` logic duplicated in store and payments
issues.description: The `getSharedCookieDomain(hostname)` function — which strips the hostname to a shared parent domain for cross-app cookie scoping — is defined twice with identical logic: once in the store app and once (as a private function) in `apps/payments/src/features/checkout/infrastructure/cartCookie.ts`. The constants `MINIMUM_DOMAIN_SEGMENTS` and `DOMAIN_SUFFIX_SEGMENT_OFFSET` are also duplicated.
issues.fix: Extract `getSharedCookieDomain` into `packages/shared/src/utils/` (alongside the cart cookie constant, see DRY-003). Both apps can then import the shared utility. This is a pure function with no app-specific dependencies, making it an ideal candidate for the shared package.

issues.title: Time conversion constants (`HOURS_PER_DAY`, `MINUTES_PER_HOUR`, etc.) defined in multiple apps
issues.description: The basic time-conversion constants `MS_PER_SECOND = 1000`, `SECONDS_PER_MINUTE = 60`, `MINUTES_PER_HOUR = 60`, `HOURS_PER_DAY = 24` are defined in `apps/admin/src/features/users/domain/constants.ts`, `apps/payments/src/features/checkout/domain/constants.ts`, and `apps/store/src/features/cart/application/cartCookiePersistence.ts`. The `packages/shared/src/constants/time.ts` file exists for time constants but only contains query stale times — it is the natural home for these universal values.
issues.fix: Add `MS_PER_SECOND`, `SECONDS_PER_MINUTE`, `MINUTES_PER_HOUR`, `HOURS_PER_DAY`, `DAYS_PER_MONTH`, `DAYS_PER_YEAR`, `MONTHS_PER_YEAR` to `packages/shared/src/constants/time.ts` and re-export them. Remove the per-app copies and import from `shared/constants/time`. Note: apply KISS — only do this if the admin and payments usages genuinely share the same semantic meaning, which they do (these are universal unit constants).

issues.title: `useSellerProfiles` hook duplicated in store and payments with near-identical logic
issues.description: Both the store and payments apps define a `useSellerProfiles(sellerIds)` hook that queries `user_profiles` for a list of seller IDs and returns a `Record<string, string>` name map. The store's version inlines the Supabase query; the payments version delegates to `fetchSellerProfiles`. The core purpose, query key, query shape, and return type are identical.
issues.fix: This hook crosses app boundaries and depends on the Supabase client, so the KISS guardrail applies — extracting it to `packages/shared` would introduce Supabase as a package dependency. The better fix is to consolidate the underlying `fetchSellerProfiles` infrastructure function in one shared location (or accept the duplication as intentional isolation between apps since the two hooks have subtle differences in fallback logic and deduplication).

issues.title: Vitest config `resolve.alias` block copy-pasted across all 6 apps
issues.description: The `resolve.alias` block mapping `@`, `@shared`, `@ui`, `@api`, `@app-components`, `shared`, `ui`, `api`, and `@monorepo/app-components` to their respective package paths is copy-pasted identically across all 6 app vitest configs. A change to the monorepo package layout requires updating all 6 files.
issues.fix: Extract a shared `createVitestAliases(__dirname)` helper into a root-level `vitest.shared.ts` (or `vitest.aliases.ts`) file. Each app's config imports and spreads it: `resolve: { alias: createVitestAliases(__dirname) }`. Alternatively, explore Vitest's built-in workspace support (`vitest.workspace.ts`) which can define shared config at the monorepo root.

patterns.name: Per-app config shim files are thin re-exports
patterns.description: Files like `apps/*/src/shared/infrastructure/config/environment.ts`, `api.ts`, and `tid.ts` are one-line re-exports from `packages/shared`. This is the correct intentional pattern (not a DRY violation) — it creates a stable import path for each app while the implementation lives in the shared package.

patterns.name: ProtectedRoute.tsx is identical across 4 apps
patterns.description: `apps/admin`, `apps/payments`, `apps/store`, and `apps/studio` each have a `ProtectedRoute.tsx` that contains exactly: `export const ProtectedRoute = createProtectedRoute(appUrls.auth)`. This is two-line boilerplate that exists because each app uses its own `appUrls` import. Given KISS, this does not warrant further abstraction.
patterns.verdict: Acceptable — KISS wins, two lines per app is not worth abstracting

patterns.name: MSW browser/server setup files are identical boilerplate
patterns.description: `apps/admin`, `apps/store`, and `apps/payments` each have `mocks/browser.ts` and `mocks/server.ts` files with identical 5-line MSW setup. These are app-scoped by design (each app has its own handler set), and KISS suggests leaving them as-is.
patterns.verdict: Acceptable — MSW setup is intentionally app-scoped
